methods.description: Monitoring of airborne Ambrosia pollen in Lublin, Poland, during 2011-2012 using the volumetric method with a Hirst-type sampler (Lanzoni VPPS 2000). The methodology followed the recommendations of the International Association for Aerobiology. Pollen was counted on slides along 4 longitudinal transects, and results were expressed as the number of pollen grains per 1 m³ of air in 24 hours (P/m³).

steps.description: Set up a Hirst-type sampler (Lanzoni VPPS 2000) at a height of 18 m on the roof of the building of the University of Life Sciences in the city centre (51 o 14'37''N; 22 o 32'25''E).
steps.searchable_summary: Set up volumetric pollen sampler at specified location and height

steps.description: Carry out monitoring of airborne Ambrosia pollen during the period of 2011 and 2012 using the deployed sampler.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect airborne pollen samples using volumetric sampler over monitoring period

steps.description: Count pollen on slides along 4 longitudinal transects with a length of 48 mm each.
steps.searchable_summary: Count pollen grains on collected slides following specified transect protocol
inputs: Counting protocol (4 longitudinal transects, 48 mm length each)

steps.description: Express the obtained results as the number of pollen grains per 1 m³ of air in 24 hours (P/m³).
steps.searchable_summary: Convert raw pollen counts to volumetric concentration (P/m³)
outputs: Airborne pollen concentration data (P/m³) for Lublin

methods.description: Monitoring of airborne Ambrosia pollen in Lviv, Ukraine, during 2011-2012 using the gravimetric method with a Durham trap. Pollen grains were counted on the entire slide area, and the data were converted using a factor of 3.2 to estimate volumetric concentration, expressed as P/m³.

steps.description: Set up a Durham trap which was placed at a height of 10 m in the city centre (49 o 50'8"N; 24 o 1'58"E).
steps.searchable_summary: Set up gravimetric pollen sampler at specified location and height

steps.description: Carry out monitoring of airborne Ambrosia pollen during the period of 2011 and 2012 using the deployed sampler.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect airborne pollen samples using gravimetric sampler over monitoring period

steps.description: Count pollen grains on the entire slide and the total slide area was 11.52 cm².
steps.searchable_summary: Count pollen grains on collected slides over the entire slide area

steps.description: Convert the data obtained by the gravimetric method using a factor of 3.2 which was calculated following the guidelines of Basett et al. [14].
steps.searchable_summary: Convert gravimetric pollen counts to estimated volumetric concentration using a conversion factor
outputs: Estimated volumetric pollen concentration data for Lviv

steps.description: Express the obtained results as the number of pollen grains per 1 m³ of air in 24 hours (P/m³).
steps.searchable_summary: Format estimated volumetric concentration as P/m³
outputs: Airborne pollen concentration data (P/m³) for Lviv

methods.description: Analysis of the correlations between airborne Ambrosia pollen concentration and meteorological factors (temperature - mean, minimum, maximum; air humidity; wind direction) for both Lublin and Lviv using Spearman's rank correlation test. Statistical analysis was performed using STA-TISTICA ver.8 and GraphPad Prism 5 software.

steps.description: Obtain airborne pollen concentration data (P/m³) for both cities.
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve processed airborne pollen concentration data for Lublin and Lviv
inputs: Airborne pollen concentration data (P/m³) for Lublin (from Method 1)
Airborne pollen concentration data (P/m³) for Lviv (from Method 2)
outputs: Pollen concentration time series data for Lublin
Pollen concentration time series data for Lviv

steps.description: Obtain meteorological data for Lublin from an automatic weather station Vantage Pro 2 located close to the pollen trap.
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve meteorological data for Lublin from local weather station
inputs: Meteorological factors (mean temp, min temp, max temp, air humidity, wind direction)
Data source (automatic weather station Vantage Pro 2)
outputs: Meteorological time series data for Lublin

steps.description: Obtain meteorological data for Lviv from the website www.meteoprog.ua/ua/fwarchive/Lviv/.
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve meteorological data for Lviv from specified website archive
inputs: Meteorological factors (mean temp, min temp, max temp, air humidity, wind direction)
outputs: Meteorological time series data for Lviv

steps.description: Calculate the correlations between airborne pollen concentration and meteorological factors for Lublin by Spearman's rank correlation test.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Spearman's rank correlation analysis between pollen and meteorological data for Lublin
inputs: Pollen concentration time series data for Lublin
Meteorological time series data for Lublin

steps.description: Calculate the correlations between airborne pollen concentration and meteorological factors for Lviv by Spearman's rank correlation test.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Spearman's rank correlation analysis between pollen and meteorological data for Lviv
inputs: Pollen concentration time series data for Lviv
Meteorological time series data for Lviv

steps.description: Use STA-TISTICA ver.8 and GraphPad Prism 5 software for the statistical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Utilize specified statistical software for analysis
